name: search
title: Search
description: A site-search landmark: a form role=search wrapping a labelled type=search input with a leading magnifier and a submit button, styled as one bordered control with a single focus ring on the input. A clear button appears once there is text; Enter submits and, with an action, the form performs a native GET/POST so search works without JavaScript. Passing suggestions upgrades it to a Base UI Autocomplete combobox with a listbox, aria-activedescendant highlight tracking, and a no-results boundary — an enhancement that never blocks submitting a free-text query.
useWhen: A site-wide or section search box in a header, hero, or results page
A single free-text query submitted to a search results page or endpoint
You want optional type-ahead suggestions without losing a plain, no-JS search form
avoidWhen: Choosing one value from a constrained list rather than searching (use a Combo Box or Select)
Filtering a list in place with no submit step (use a labelled Input with your own filtering)
Collecting a non-query field in a larger form (use an Input inside a Field)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the search input; Tab again reaches the clear button (when present) and the submit button
Enter submits the search from the input
Space or Enter activates the focused submit or clear button
Escape clears the highlighted suggestion / closes the suggestions list in the autocomplete variant
Down Arrow opens the suggestions and moves the highlight to the next suggestion; Up Arrow moves to the previous one (autocomplete variant)
The highlighted suggestion is tracked with aria-activedescendant while focus stays in the input (autocomplete variant)
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (input, clear, and submit each fill the 44px field height)
notes: The input has a real, visually-hidden <label> (default "Search"); the submit button has an accessible name from its visible text, or from aria-label when rendered icon-only.
The whole control sits in a role=search landmark form; with an `action` it submits natively (GET/POST) so search works with JavaScript disabled, and onSearch runs alongside it.

One border wraps the leading icon, input, clear, and submit, and a single focus ring appears scoped to the input (focus-within cancelled, then re-added via has-[[data-slot=input]:focus]) so a focused button rings only itself.

Non-color redundancy: a real border is kept in every state, error state adds a thicker inset ring (not color alone), and the leading/submit magnifier uses currentColor so it survives forced-colors mode; suggestion highlight adds font-weight, not just a fill.

The autocomplete variant is a strict enhancement: with no `suggestions` it is a plain search form, and a free-text query can always be submitted whether or not it matches a suggestion. The Base UI combobox is wrapped in AmbientDirection so its portalled popup follows dir=rtl.
